question: Can a lower-temporary PyTorch Newton-Schulz polynomial workspace reduce the batched expert-Muon optimizer wall?

patch_tested.change: Replace torch.add(gram.mul(b), gram @ gram, alpha=c) with an in-place polynomial workspace on gram @ gram, and replace (matrix / norm).to(...) with matrix.div(norm).to(...).

ns1_chunk4.read: The retained-relevant NS1 path is slightly slower and does not reduce peak memory.

ns5_phase.read: The high-NS diagnostic path improves only about 0.18%, far below an e2e promotion threshold.

decision.read: This PyTorch-level workspace reduction does not move the relevant NS1 batched expert-Muon path and gives only a tiny NS5 diagnostic improvement. Keep the existing BatchedMuonExpertParams implementation, retain AdamW plus attention-only activation checkpointing as throughput-best, and focus next on MoE/EP helper VJP, weighted combine/scatter, and sparse SharedKV backward.
